tender.title: SUPPORTED ACCOMMODATION FOR YOUNG PEOPLE BLOCK CONTRACTS FOR UNACCOMPANIED ASYLUM-SEEKING CHILDREN (NEW ARRIVALS) (LOT 2)
tender.description:
This Requirement (RQ1043022) is specifically for block contracts for Newly Arrived UASC (Lot 2). If you would like to bid for 16+ (Lot 1) please see (RQ1042968) and if you would like to bid for 18+ (Lot 3) please see (RQ1043045). You are welcome to Bid for one or more lots.

In order to submit an Offer for this Requirement, you must be able to make properties and support services available for exclusive use by the Council from 1 January 2024. 

You must also: 
1. Read all the attached documents carefully. 
2. Provide a response to each of the Profile questions. 
3. Please ensure you respond to all Offer questions. 
4. Complete and upload the Pricing Schedule (please make reference to council's indicative budget in Invitation to Tender Document) 
5. Complete and upload the Standard Tender Questionnaire. 
6. Complete/return/upload the TUPE Confidentiality Agreement (MKCC will then provide further TUPE information) 

Any queries you might have must be raised by using the Requirement Messages facility by 16 October 2023. The Council will commit to providing a response by 20 October 2023. 

Please Note: Offers will only be evaluated after the closing date shown in the Requirement. As a result, the Offer Rank and Score that is shown whilst the Requirement remains open should be disregarded in this instance. 
- Price Weight: 40% 
- Quality Review Weight: 60% 

Please note the council requires properties to be within Milton Keynes and the borders of Milton Keynes.
tender.procurementMethodDetails: Call-off from a dynamic purchasing system

tender.title: G&R/HLN/Emergency Accommodation Framework
tender.description:
G&R/HLN/Emergency Accommodation Framework\\r\r
\\r\r
Bristol City Council in collaboration with South Gloucestershire Council intends to let an Open Framework for the provision of its Emergency Accommodation Service. \\r\r
\\r\r
The procurement process being used for this project is that of a revised Dynamic Purchasing System (DPS), referred to as an "Open Framework".  The Open Framework will be open for tenderers to join throughout the life of the contract.\\r\r
\\r\r
This process has been undertaken on a voluntary basis with the Services being exempt from Part 2 of the Public Contracts Regulations 2015 by virtue of Regulation 10(1)(a), but with a view to attracting as many Service Providers as possible.\\r\r
\\r\r
This is an award notice relating to the establishment of this Framework. \\r\r
\\r\r
Providers wishing to apply can do so via - https://procontract.due-north.com/Advert?advertId=e5643a26-ce53-ee11-8124-005056b64545\\r\r

tender.title: Supported Accommodation Pseudo-Dynamic Purchasing System
tender.description:
Procurement to establish a Pseudo-Dynamic Purchasing System (PDPS) for Supported Accommodation Services for Young People in Care aged 16 - 18.

This PDPS will be split into three Lots:

Lot 1 Supported Accommodation and Support for Eligible and Relevant Young People in Care Aged 16 and 17

Lot 2 - Supported Accommodation and Support for Separated Migrant Children and /or Formally Separated Migrant Children with No Recourse to Public Funds

Lot 3 - Block Contract Call Offs for Supported Accommodation and Support.

Providers must have Ofsted Registration for the Supported Accommodation (England) Regulations 2023 (CQC Registration is not accepted) and accommodation within a 20 mile radius of PE2 8TY.

tender.title: Extra Care and Housing Related Support Service
tender.description:
The London Borough of Merton is inviting tenders from providers with relevant skills and experience to provide Extra Care and Housing Related Support services to customers aged 55 years or over at two Extra Care Housing Supported Schemes: Lot 1 - Pantiles House, Merton Park (33 flats) and, Lot 2 - Trellis House, Colliers Wood (42 flats) \r
\r
It is the intention of the Council to award either a single contract to a provider covering both Pantiles House and Trellis House or two contracts to two providers covering one Service each.

documents.description: Awarded contract notice on Contracts Finder

tender.title: Community Supported Living - Accommodation Care and Support - Lincolnshire
tender.description:
Community Supported Living Care and Support with Accommodation - Lincolnshire\r
\r
Lincolnshire County Council will be undertaking a procurement in line with Regulations 74-76 of the Public Contract Regulations 2015 (PCR), the Light Touch Regime, and pursuant to Regulation 76(7) of the PCR, for a community supported living service in Lincolnshire.\r
Please note that appendices A-E contain sensitive information. These documents will be made available to CQC registered organisations only. Please message through ProContract and these documents will be sent to your organisation if it is CQC registered.\r
The time limits for receipt of tenders have been accelerated for reasons of significant urgency in the sourcing of a suitable care and support solution.'

documents.description: Awarded contract notice on Contracts Finder

tender.title: Provision of Support and Accommodation for Adults Experiencing Homelessness and Multiple Disadvantage - Watford 2
tender.description:
This is an Award Notice for the Provision of Support and Accommodation for Adults Experiencing Homelessness and Multiple Disadvantage - Watford 2. This is a direct award because competition is absent for technical reasons. 

The Service will be free at the point of use and will support single adults aged 18 and over experiencing homelessness and multiple disadvantage in Stevenage. The provider will make available 80 Units of support combination of accommodation based support and floating support as needed to be used flexibly 

Services will be focussed to target support to the following groups 
•	High/complex/multiple needs: i.e. Long-term rough sleepers or people encountering repeat homelessness and requiring intensive support through Housing First or supported housing with high-level of management and support. 

•	Medium/high needs: people with fluctuating levels of support, who are experiencing homelessness/rough sleeping and/or who have health, care and support needs best met through a housing-led placement with sufficient support. 

This Service will deliver a strengths based and trauma informed approach to support. The keyworker will listen to and understand the person's priorities, focussing on their personal goals and how they can be supported to live a full, healthy, active, and independent life.
tender.procurementMethodDetails: Negotiated procedure without prior publication (above threshold)

documents.description: Awarded contract notice on Contracts Finder

tender.title: 23-131 - Work Order 3 - Provision of Accommodation with Support for Homeless Households
tender.description:
The service will work in line with the Specification for Lot 2 of the Housing Related Support Flexible Purchasing System. The people supported by this service will be people aged 18 plus who are experiencing homelessness.  However, people under the age of 25 should be considered for the young persons pathway unless this pathway is more suitable in the first instance.  
The service will work in a trauma-informed way to help people using the service to identify the root cause of homelessness and identify barriers to move-on, producing an achievable plan of support. 
The service will provide a pathway to move on and set out a plan to move on within 6-9 months and will support through the Council's allocation process or sourcing alternative suitable accommodation within the private rented sector or other Social Housing Provision, as suitable.

Additional information: Route to Market
Social Value

User / Company

tender.title: 23-130 - Work Order 2 - Floating Support (non-accommodation based)
tender.description:
The service will work in line with the Specification for Lot 1 of the Housing Related Support Flexible Purchasing System. The people supported by this service will be any household type, size and tenure. The service will typically be for people aged 18 and above who are at risk of becoming homeless. If the person is aged 18-25 and needs are more suited to a younger person's specialist service, the service should support access into the Young Persons Housing Related Support provision. If the person is a Council tenant, the service will engage with the Council's Tenancy Support Service to determine the best placed service to meet the individual's needs.

Additional information: Route to Market
Social Value

documents.description: Awarded contract notice on Contracts Finder

tender.title: Rough Sleeper Housing Support Services - Integrated Floating Support - AWARD
tender.description:
In accordance with paragraph 3.4 of the instructions to Participate (ITP) Suppliers who have been successful in being admitted onto the Framework are being invited to submit tenders for  the following initial call off contracts for Integrated Floating support.
      
      The aim of the service is to support people with a history of rough sleeping, or at risk of rough sleeping, to make positive moves towards maintaining settled accommodation and achieving an improved quality of life 
      
      The purpose of the service is to enable people, often entrenched in a rough sleeping lifestyle, to take up the opportunity of a more settled way of life within their own accommodation. The service will enable people to establish and maintain a tenancy and wherever possible make a successful transition to independent living regardless of initial level of need.
      
      Support will primarily be delivered to people in their own accommodation in the community which can include temporary accommodation
      
      The proposed terms of the contract is for the initial period of 3 years with a possible extension of 2 years making a total term of 5 years
      
      The annual value of the contract is £687,700 per year
      
      Suppliers should submit their tenders by the tender closing date.

documents.description: Awarded contract notice on Contracts Finder

tender.title: Framework for the Provision of Temporary Emergency Accommodation
tender.description:
The procurement documents were available for unrestricted and full direct access, free of charge, at: https://uk.eu-supply.com/login.asp?B=YORTENDER.

Queries and Tenders were to be submitted electronically via this portal.

Kirklees Council sought suitable providers of temporary accommodation to offer flexible nightly paid bed and breakfast hotel accommodation or nightly paid self- contained / self-catering accommodation for short notice and emergency accommodation needs on an ad hoc basis under a Framework Agreement.

The Framework consists of four Lots:

•	Lot 1. Nightly Paid In-Borough Accommodation
•	Lot 2. Nightly Paid Out of Borough Accommodation
•	Lot 3. Self-Contained In-Borough Accommodation
•	Lot 4. Self Contained Out of Borough Accommodation - this Lot was not awarded

The commencement date for the Framework Agreement is 3rd April  2024. The anticipated expiry date for the Framework Agreement is 2nd April 2026, with the option to extend for up to two (2) further twelve (12) month periods up to 2nd April 2028.
